1.11a: Contact details are provided on the letter of instruction including address, fax, email, and phone number.
Standard documents are available at www.natwest.com/conveyancers.
For uncompleted property purchases, use the LMS portal for contact.
General queries should be sent via email.
For Offset Flexible Mortgages, contact the firm specified on the letter of instruction.
1.11b: Contact procedures are identical to section 1.11a.
1.7: The lender does not allow the borrower and mortgagor to be different entities.
1.14: A firm may act if the person or an immediate family member is the seller, provided the lender is notified via the contact details in 1.11a.
1.15: A firm may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or an immediate family member is the borrower, unless a separate partner or fee earner of equal standing acts for the lender.
3.1.3: Do not provide the seller's solicitor details unless specifically requested by the lender.
3.1.4: Notify the lender via 1.11a if the seller is unrepresented to receive a decision on whether to proceed.
3.1.5: Acceptable identity documents include old-style UK driving licences, Construction Industry tax exemption certificates (Inland Revenue), and Inland Revenue Tax Notifications.
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor firm is not required unless requested.
3.2.4: Notify the lender via 1.11a if the seller lacks legal representation.
4.1: A copy of the valuation report is provided if a physical interior inspection was conducted.
If no physical inspection was conducted, the report is not shared and the borrower must answer property-related questions.
4.5a: Contact the point specified in 1.11a for re-inspection requests.
4.5b: The certificate of title should be sent as detailed in 1.11.
5.1.1: Report properties owned by the seller for less than 6 months via 1.11a.
5.2.1: Report instances where the seller is not the registered owner via 1.11a.
5.4.4: Environmental and contaminated land reports should not be sent to the lender.
Borrowers must disclose environmental risks to their insurer.
Report HS2 proximity only if the property is within 300 meters.
Mining interpretive reports are not required; however, if a mining entry is within 20 meters, the property must be insurable on normal terms.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the agent has adequate professional indemnity insurance and a clear Certificate of Title can be issued.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted if it provides adequate protection and the title is certified as good and marketable.
5.5.3a: Report breaches or unsatisfied conditions via 1.11a.
5.5.3b: Submit original or copy of planning permission after completion.
5.5.3c: Submit original or copy of building regulation consents after completion.
5.5.3d: Submit certificates of lawful use or development after completion.
5.5.4: Report restrictions affecting value or marketability via 1.11a.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if positive covenants for maintenance, repair, and insurance are shared and enforceable.
Maximum Loan to Value (LTV) for flying freeholds is 90%.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are accepted if positive covenants for maintenance, repair, and insurance are shared and enforceable.
Maximum Loan to Value (LTV) for freehold flats is 90%.
5.7.1c: Report accepted freehold flats or flying freeholds via 1.11a.
5.8.1: Buildings converted into up to four flats are accepted if the borrower occupies one flat, owns the freehold (individually or with another owner), and other flats are on long leases.
5.8.5: Leasehold flats where the borrower and other leaseholder own each other's freehold reversions are accepted if security includes both the leasehold flat and the freehold reversion of the other flat.
5.9.1: Commonhold cases must be referred to the Bank individually.
5.13.1: Unconditional, non-repayable gifts from third parties (excluding vendors) are permitted without additional request.
Second charges to protect giftor interests are permitted provided they are non-repayable and a deed of postponement is in place for the lender's first charge.
Report any repayments related to a second charge.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term must be the mortgage term plus 30 years.
5.14.9: Ground rents making a lease an Assured Shorthold Tenancy do not need reporting if the lease is varied or indemnity insurance is obtained.
Onerous ground rents must be reported, including increases exceeding RPI, doubling in less than 25 years, or fixed increases above inflation.
New residential leases under the Leasehold Reform Act 2022 must be restricted to a peppercorn rent.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is accepted for unsatisfactory lease terms, preferably after attempting to vary the lease with the landlord.
5.14.12: Ground rent and service charge receipts must be sent after completion.
5.14.13: A receipted copy of notice or evidence of service must be sent after completion.
5.14.15a: Report absentee or insolvent landlords via 1.11a.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for an absent or insolvent landlord is accepted.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates must be sent after completion.
5.15.2c: A signed blank stock transfer form must be sent after completion.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association must be sent after completion.
5.17.5a: Certified copies of Power of Attorney must be sent after completion.
5.17.5b: Certified copies of the statutory declaration of non-revocation for Power of Attorney must be sent after completion.
5.19.1: Proposals for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership must be reported via 1.11a.
5.20.1: Leases of roof space for solar panels not meeting UK Finance minimum requirements must be reported via 1.11a.
5.20.3: Confirmation of the existing lender's consent is required for solar panel roof space leases.
5.20.4: Disclose any existing Green Deal Plans via 1.11a.
6.4.4: Incentives totaling more than 5% of the purchase price must be reported.
The UK Finance Disclosure Form should not be sent to the lender.
6.6.1: Notify the lender via 1.11a only if the mortgage offer does not already reflect that the property is let or to be let.
6.6.3: Counterpart or certified copies of tenancy agreements are not required.
6.6.4: The lender does not lend on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO).
6.7.1: Accepted warranty schemes: NHBC, Zurich Municipal, Building Life Plans, Global Home Warranties, Checkmate/Castle 10, Build Assure New Homes Structural Defects Insurance, Premier Guarantee, The Q Policy, ABC+, One Guarantee, Ark Residential New Build Latent Defects Insurance.
6.7.4: Properties without a warranty scheme require monitoring by a professional consultant and a Professional Consultant’s Certificate in the CML form.
6.7.6: The Professional Consultant's Certificate must be sent after completion.
6.10.2: Retentions are released to the Borrower unless written instructions specify a third party.
7.3: Consent is required for all occupants aged 17 and over, excluding the borrower's children under 26.
For Lifetime Mortgages, spouses and civil partners must be joint owners and mortgagors.
Post-March 2, 2011 instructions use a combined Mortgage Deed/Consent; pre-March 2, 2011 instructions require a separate Deed of Consent and Postponement returned at completion.
8.1: Occupants executing a consent to mortgage must be recommended to obtain independent legal advice and be provided with the Standard Mortgage Conditions.
9.1: The indemnity insurance policy must be sent after completion.
9.2: Indemnity insurance must equal the property value.
10.2a: Conveyancer fees can be added to the loan and transferred separately on completion day if requested by the borrower.
10.2b: A minimum of 7 working days' notice is required for the mortgage advance.
10.3: A CHAPS fee is deducted from the mortgage advance.
10.7: Delayed completion funds must be returned by CHAPS to Sort Code 60-06-58, Account 98090097 by the following working day.
The return must include any conveyancer fee funds released with the advance.
10.11: Interest on returned advances is charged daily at the mortgage rate from the date of release until re-credited.
14.1.4: A CH2 application is required only for Flexible Choice mortgages with a Reserve facility.
14.1.5: The original mortgage deed must be returned to the lender after Land Registry registration; a certified copy is sent to the registry.
14.2.2: Post-completion documents include official copies of the Register, title deeds, and any guarantees referenced in valuation reports.
16.1.1: Title documents contact: Deeds Dispatch, PO Box 123, Greenock, PA15 1EF. Tel: 01475 551256. DX 599600 Greenock 3.
16.3.7b: The attestation clause must state: Signed and delivered as a Deed for and on behalf of the Lender by a duly authorised signatory.
16.4.2: Proposed tenancy copies must be sent unless they are Assured Shorthold Tenancies.
17.1.1: Redemption statement contact: UBS Statement Team, PO Box 123, Greenock, PA15 1EF. Tel: 08457 212600. DX 599600 Greenock 3.
17.2.1b: Discharge is sent via a DS1 form or directly to the Land Registry.
